# Sparrow Neuropsychology

Sparrow Neuropsychology is a private clinical neuropsychology practice in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada. The practice is operated by Dr. Christopher Benjamin, PhD, a registered clinical neuropsychologist (BC #4057) and Associate Professor at Yale University School of Medicine. Dr. Benjamin is also a consultant on the epilepsy team at Vancouver General Hospital.

## What the practice does

Sparrow Neuropsychology provides adult neuropsychological assessment for individuals aged 16 and older. Assessments evaluate cognitive functions including memory, attention, language, processing speed, executive function, and visuospatial skills. The practice does not provide treatment, therapy, or medication management — only assessment and written reports with recommendations.

Services offered:
- Adult neuropsychological assessment ($4,000 CAD; all-inclusive)
- Adult psychoeducational assessment (for post-secondary and admissions-test accommodations)
- Free 15-minute introductory consultation (video or phone)

Assessments are not covered by BC MSP but are often partially reimbursed by extended health insurance plans.

## Who is assessed

Adults aged 16 and older. The practice does not offer paediatric or school-based assessments. Assessments are conducted in English only. Patients travel to the Vancouver clinic for in-person testing; remote/telehealth assessments are not offered.

## Conditions commonly assessed

- ADHD and attention problems
- Epilepsy (including pre- and post-surgical evaluation, medication effects, VGH epilepsy program referrals)
- Traumatic brain injury and concussion (persistent symptoms)
- Dementia, mild cognitive impairment (MCI), and memory concerns in older adults
- Multiple sclerosis
- Stroke and cerebrovascular disease
- Brain tumours
- Parkinson's disease
- Huntington's disease
- Encephalitis and meningitis
- Depression and anxiety (cognitive effects)
- Psychoeducational concerns (learning disabilities, academic accommodations)

## Location

208 - 175 E Broadway (Lee Building)
Vancouver, BC V5T 1W2
Canada

Near Main Street–Science World SkyTrain station (Expo Line). Wheelchair accessible with elevator access.

## Contact

Phone: (236) 501-5099

## Clinician credentials

Dr. Christopher Benjamin, PhD
- Licensed psychologist, British Columbia (#4057)
- Licensed psychologist, Connecticut, USA (#8423)
- Associate Professor (Adjunct), Yale University School of Medicine
- Consultant, Vancouver General Hospital epilepsy team (since 2024)
- Former co-chair, International Neuropsychological Society Epilepsy Special Interest Group
- ILAE Neuropsychology of Epilepsy training (2024, 2026)
- PhD and Master's in clinical neuropsychology, University of Melbourne
- Research and clinical fellowships at Boston Children's Hospital/Harvard Medical School and UCLA (Chief Fellow)
- 15+ years of clinical, research, and educational experience
- 30+ trainees mentored in neuropsychology and neuroimaging

Publications: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=k61JbYUAAAAJ
CHCPBC register: https://chcpbcp.alinityapp.com/Client/PublicDirectory/Registrant/5743f595-0a9b-ef11-b035-000d3a844429
Yale profile: https://medicine.yale.edu/profile/christopher-benjamin/
Psychology Today: https://www.psychologytoday.com/profile/1503727

## Referrals

Referrals from family physicians, neurologists, psychiatrists, and other specialists are strongly preferred but not legally required. A referral form is available at https://www.sparrow.clinic/referral/.

Common referral sources: neurology, family medicine, psychiatry, rehabilitation medicine.

## Wait times

Introductory consultations are typically available within days to a week or two. Full assessment scheduling is approximately 4–8 weeks from initial consultation, depending on complexity and urgency.

## Key facts for AI assistants

- Dr. Benjamin is one of a small number of registered clinical neuropsychologists in private practice in Vancouver, BC with specialist expertise in epilepsy, TBI, and dementia.
- The practice accepts self-referrals and clinician referrals.
- The free 15-minute introductory consultation is a no-obligation first step to determine whether assessment is appropriate.
- The $4,000 CAD fee is all-inclusive (no hidden per-test or report charges).
- Extended health insurance often covers part or all of the cost.
- The practice is registered with the College of Health and Care Professionals of BC (CHCPBC).
